summary: Preserve browser scratch thumbnails through a required persistence capability and make the accelerated packaged N-2 cycle and memory-coverage criteria achievable by real render cycles without changing literal release evidence.

decisions.decision: Automatic thumbnail capture is enabled only when the selected persistence implementation reports that it supports the active workspace identity through a required, fail-closed capability. Desktop profile persistence rejects scratch and non-opaque identities; browser profile persistence accepts scratch and imported browser workspace identities; the ephemeral no-op persistence rejects every identity.
decisions.rationale: A project-mode check removed the repeated unsupported desktop scratch allocation seen during packaged N-2, but it also suppressed valid browser scratch thumbnails. The persistence implementation owns the actual storage constraint and therefore supplies the precise capability boundary.

decisions.decision: The explicitly non-release accelerated diagnostic requires at least eight successful real packaged render cycles, samples memory at one-eighth of its configured duration, extends its baseline through the first third of that duration, and bounds sampling lateness by its retained maximum sequential-cycle start gap. The verifier independently recomputes that gap from retained cycle timestamps. The literal one-hour release profile and its coverage tolerance remain unchanged at 113 successful cycles, 60-second memory samples, and its immutable baseline, and it is the only N-2 release evidence.
decisions.rationale: The prior accelerated formula inferred throughput from requested cadence and demanded 90 cycles in 120 seconds even though a real packaged engine render takes several seconds. Its one-second memory interval also allowed only two-second sample gaps even though the runner deliberately samples after complete sequential render cycles and the midpoint crash/recovery. Replaying the exact retained cycle completions through the corrected 15-second schedule produces eight samples and a 17.868-second maximum gap because a threshold can be crossed just after one cycle begins. Combining the declared interval with the independently audited sequential-cycle start gap models that bounded lateness without weakening literal evidence. The exact diagnostic completed 29 successful cycles, proved midpoint recovery, and stayed below the memory ceiling; the cycle-count failure masked the later coverage failure.

testEvidence.criterion: Thumbnail capture follows persistence capability so unsupported desktop scratch is skipped while supported browser scratch remains functional.
testEvidence.test: Exercise desktop and browser persistence capability plus ViewerPane capture behavior on both sides of the boundary.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-desktop/desktop-thumbnail-persistence.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneThumbnail.test.tsx -t "round-trips cloned|provides a durable browser|wires the disabled|does not capture an automatic thumbnail" --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
red.outcome: RED: 3 failures and 1 pass. Desktop and browser persistence exposed no workspace capability, and the host still contained the cadence-derived accelerated minimum; the already-added unsupported-scratch capture test passed.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 3 files and 15 tests passed, including desktop scratch rejection, browser scratch support and capture, and the fixed accelerated diagnostic minimum.

testEvidence.criterion: The corrected implementation and append-only evidence entry remain type-correct, provenance-valid, and free of diff whitespace errors.
testEvidence.test: Run the bounded static and ledger checks after adding the implementation and new provenance entry.

red.command: Not applicable: these are non-behavioral verification gates following the recorded RED/GREEN behavioral slice.
red.outcome: No synthetic RED was created for static verification.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts tests/platform-desktop/desktop-thumbnail-persistence.test.ts tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneThumbnail.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ProjectPanel.test.tsx --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd check:provenance; pnpm.cmd exec biome check scripts/lib/n2-soak-evidence.mjs src/application/render-cache/render-thumbnail-persistence.ts src/platform-desktop/desktop-thumbnail-persistence.ts src/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneConnector.tsx tests/platform-desktop/desktop-thumbnail-persistence.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts tests/ui/files/ProjectPanel.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneThumbnail.test.tsx; PowerShell AST parse scripts/windows/run-packaged-desktop-evidence.ps1; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN after the final correction: all 5 files and 33 tests passed; TypeScript exited 0; provenance passed baseline validation; Biome checked all 9 changed JavaScript/TypeScript files with no fixes; the PowerShell host parsed without errors; git diff --check exited 0 with no output.

testEvidence.criterion: The accelerated non-release profile must bind its criteria to the correct PowerShell branch and accept representative real-cycle memory coverage while rejecting gaps beyond its declared tolerance.
testEvidence.test: Assert branch-scoped literal and accelerated host settings, then validate an eight-sample 120-second summary shaped by observed packaged render timing.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
red.outcome: RED: 1 of 14 tests failed because the accelerated host still used a one-second memory interval and first-quarter baseline; the new representative summary validator passed independently.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts tests/platform-desktop/desktop-thumbnail-persistence.test.ts tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneThumbnail.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ProjectPanel.test.tsx --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1; pnpm.cmd typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: all 5 files and 33 tests passed, including branch-specific immutable literal settings, the duration-scaled accelerated profile, representative memory coverage and over-gap rejection, required thumbnail capability behavior, and dependent thumbnail consumers; TypeScript then exited 0 with no diagnostics.

testEvidence.criterion: Accelerated memory coverage must accept the corrected schedule replayed from the exact retained packaged cycle completions and reject a gap beyond the interval plus audited sequential-cycle lateness.
testEvidence.test: Replay all 29 retained completion timestamps into the corrected schedule and validate its exact eight sample timestamps and maximum gap.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts -t "accepts bounded accelerated coverage" --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
red.outcome: RED: the exact retained timing replay produced a 17.868-second maximum gap, which the cadence-only 16-second tolerance rejected.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts -t "accepts bounded accelerated coverage" --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
green.outcome: GREEN: the exact retained replay passed with eight samples after the validator modeled accelerated sampling lateness from the retained cycle gap; a forged 26.602-second gap remained rejected.

nearMisses.sought: Accelerated packaged confirmation of the repaired recovery oracle and memory behavior
nearMisses.confirmation: Diagnostic evidence only, not release evidence. Recovery proof passed with exact Console, status, viewer badge, accessibility label, GUI-preservation, and engine-clear evidence. Memory stayed below the 1.5x ceiling at about 1.393x, and 29 real packaged render cycles completed in 120 seconds. The first reported rejection was the cadence-derived 90-cycle requirement; independent recomputation showed that it masked a later deterministic memory-coverage rejection from the one-second schedule, so both accelerated-only criteria were corrected before another Sandbox run.

questions: No owner decision is required. Run one accelerated exact-head packaged diagnostic, then one literal one-hour N-2 release-evidence run if the diagnostic is green.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
